title: Backlog Triage — 5 candidates from Immigration Concierge scan
description: Dev Clone scanned the registered codebase (v0.2.1) and surfaced 5 code-grounded backlog candidates. Accept the ones to implement as focused PRs, reject (with reason — rejected categories are suppressed after 3 rejections), or defer to next scan. No implementation runs until you accept, and dev_config.json must be added for the PR lifecycle.

title: Triage BL-001 — Decouple case-file UI from hardcoded data.ts fixture
description: HIGH-priority architecture candidate, verified against the codebase: 3 components + 1 test import the single-case fixture from data.ts at module scope, blocking all multi-case work (Week 5 attorney review UI, evidence-vault epics). Proposed: introduce a getCaseFile(caseId) data-source boundary, keep data.ts as the seed behind it, preserve ADR 0002 status-safety invariant. No implementation will start without your accept.

title: Release event repo does not match wired codebase (ai-bookkeeper → Immigration Paperwork)
description: A release.published event for repo 'ai-bookkeeper' v0.3.0 (ledger/currency features) was delivered to a Docs Steward whose codebase connector points at 'Immigration Paperwork' (Meridian, an O-1 visa app). The features in the release do not exist in the wired repo (verified via git log, grep, tags, and missing docs/releases/v0.3.0.md). No docs were edited to avoid fabricating changes. A routing/wiring correction is needed.
